rationale.R1.2: A broad request to check the artifact centrally includes whether the argument from alert-stream motivation to benchmark contributions is internally coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation invites checking whether performance and benchmark claims are supported by the reported datasets, metrics, and comparison statements in the excerpt.
rationale.R1.4: Because the artifact makes explicit first-benchmark and improvement claims, novelty assessment is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: A general check of a paper excerpt normally includes sentence-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.R1.7: The excerpt relies on named prior work and baseline selection, making citation coverage and attribution central to the check.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of this broad check must preserve uncertainty about what can be verified from the excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.2: The checker must avoid endorsing firstness, improvement, or baseline claims beyond the evidence available.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility is limited to checking the provided paper artifact rather than executing edits or external validation unless requested.
rationale.RX.4: The check should avoid enabling misleading scientific claims or unsupported benchmark positioning.
rationale.RX.5: Traceability is required so any responsibility taken can be tied back to specific claims and statements in the artifact.
